decks.notes: The deck focuses on generating a large amount of mana quickly using cards like Pyretic Ritual and Ruby Medallion, while utilizing Ral, Monsoon Mage and Hex Magic to control the board. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces necessary to win the game, such as Past in Flames and Grapeshot, and use cards like Wish and Reckless Impulse to find them efficiently. The deck also includes ways to dig through the library and recycle important spells, ensuring consistency and resilience in the face of disruption.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's actions and maintaining board presence through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Teferi, Time Raveler. It also utilizes card advantage and card selection tools like Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive to ensure a steady stream of resources. The deck aims to stall the game until it can establish a dominant position and overwhelm the opponent with its powerful spells and planeswalker abilities.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and burn spells. The key strategy revolves around efficiently dealing damage to the opponent while maintaining board presence with creatures like Voice of Victory and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer. Utilizing cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge for direct damage, as well as Ajani, Nacatl Pariah for creature buffs, this deck aims to quickly close out games before the opponent can stabilize. Blood Moon is included to disrupt opponent's mana base and gain a tempo advantage.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to quickly assemble a board presence and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and powerful artifact synergy. By combining artifacts like Urza's Saga and Mox Opal with efficient threats like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary, the deck aims to apply pressure early and disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage through cards like Mishra's Bauble and Preordain. With a mix of disruptive elements and resilient threats, this deck aims to establish a dominant board presence and close out games quickly.

This deck focuses on quickly casting artifacts and utilizing them to generate card advantage and disrupt the opponent's game plan. It relies heavily on synergies between artifacts, such as sacrificing and recurring them with cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Arcbound Ravager. The combination of cheap artifacts and powerful abilities allows the deck to apply pressure early on and maintain board presence throughout the game. Additionally, the deck includes disruptive elements like Pithing Needle and Tormod's Crypt to disrupt opponents' strategies and protect its own game plan.

This deck focuses on combining aggressive plays with blink effects to control the board and create powerful synergy between creatures and spells. Using cards like Ephemerate and Flickerwisp to repeatedly trigger powerful ETB effects, creatures like Solitude and Psychic Frog can remove threats while generating card advantage. With a mix of aggro threats like Overlord of the Balemurk and control elements like Prismatic Ending, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a versatile and synergistic game plan.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly reanimate big threats such as Ulamog, the Defiler and Atraxa, Grand Unifier using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Faithless Looting. The deck also utilizes instant speed interaction like Consign to Memory and Fatal Push to control the board. Psychic Frog and Emperor of Bones provide additional value and disruption. The deck aims to combo off and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and a fast pace of play, utilizing graveyard interaction and reanimation spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on using reanimation spells like Persist and Unearth to bring powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones back from the graveyard to the battlefield. Thoughtseize and Inquisition of Kozilek help disrupt the opponent's game plan while Faithless Looting and Thought Scour provide card selection and graveyard filling. Fatal Push helps deal with opposing threats and Psychic Frog provides additional card draw. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's mana base with land destruction spells like Cleansing Wildfire and Field of Ruin, while controlling the board with removal spells like Path to Exile and Wrath of God. The key strategy is to slow down the opponent's game plan and strip away their resources, eventually overwhelming them with powerful threats like Solitude and Wrath of the Skies for the win. The companion Kaheera, the Orphanguard provides additional value and synergy with the deck's creature base, enhancing its control elements.

The key concept of this Boros Ponza control deck is to disrupt the opponent's mana base and resources through land destruction while maintaining control of the game with removal spells and counters. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by generating card advantage with cards like Cleansing Wildfire and Erode, while also having access to powerful finishers like Price of Freedom and Wrath of the Skies. By utilizing cards like Orim's Chant and Isochron Scepter, the deck can also lock down the opponent's spells and prevent them from mounting a comeback.

The deck focuses on maintaining a fast and aggressive playstyle through efficient creatures and direct damage spells. It also includes some control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck utilizes a blend of creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, along with burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm, to apply pressure on the opponent. Additionally, cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ranger-Captain of Eos provide additional utility and support for the aggressive strategy. The deck also includes some land destruction elements with cards like Blood Moon to disrupt the opponent's mana base.

The main goal of this Amulet Titan combo deck is to quickly ramp up mana and play Primeval Titan to fetch powerful lands like Urza's Saga, Boseiju, Who Endures, and Vesuva. With Amulet of Vigor in play, the deck can generate large amounts of mana and cast game-winning spells like Scapeshift to create an army of Elemental tokens or to find key lands like Gruul Turf and Tolaria West. The deck also utilizes cards like Simic Growth Chamber and Arboreal Grazer to accelerate mana production and maintain tempo, while utilizing toolbox cards like Green Sun's Zenith to find specific creatures as needed.

The deck focuses on utilizing Allosaurus Rider in combination with various cards like Eldritch Evolution, Neoform, and Summoner's Pact to quickly bring out powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant. The deck also includes cards like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer to protect the combo and disrupt opponent's strategies. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces as quickly as possible to overwhelm the opponent and secure a win through a powerful creature or combo finish.

The deck focuses on playing low-cost artifacts and using them to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a combination of spells and creatures. By utilizing cards like Mishra's Bauble and Memnite to generate early advantage and cards like Urza's Saga and Spirebluff Canal to generate late-game value, the deck aims to maintain a consistent tempo and put pressure on the opponent from the start. With a mix of card draw, removal, and threats like Pinnacle Emissary and Salvage Titan, the deck is designed to be an aggressive and versatile force in the Modern format.

The deck focuses on combining instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance with powerful creatures like Griselbrand to quickly bring them onto the battlefield. The strategy involves using cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up the combo. Additionally, cards like Ephemerate and Faithful Mending provide card advantage and graveyard manipulation. The deck also includes disruptive creatures like Solitude to control the board and finish the game with powerful threats like Atraxa, Grand Unifier.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with efficient creatures and burn spells. Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride provide early threats that can chip away at the opponent's life total. Seasoned Pyromancer and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah offer card advantage and additional threats. Goblin Bombardment can clear the way for attacks or finish off the opponent. The deck also includes some land destruction to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, this deck aims to be aggressive and proactive, closing out games before the opponent can stabilize.
